justification: Bird clearly deflects: business_rules, acceptance_criteria, and edge_cases are all empty arrays. The escalation explicitly flags this as out-of-scope and recommends redirecting to a general-purpose agent. The response notes this is technical/CS content, not domain content. While the output still populates the full JSON schema (domain_analysis, confidence, business_impact fields), all substantive domain sections are empty or N/A, and the escalation serves as the clear deflection mechanism.

trials.justification: Bird correctly identifies the question as out-of-scope and escalates, but still populates the full domain_analysis schema with empty/N-A fields, sets a confidence.level, and produces a structured business_impact section — all of which the rubric's pass criteria require to be absent. The response is not concise; it's a full JSON envelope with every field populated (even if with 'no impact' values). The escalation itself is well-written and clearly deflects, which prevents a fail.

Business context accurately identifies this as a textbook CS question
confidence.level is set to 99 and fully populated — rubric fail mode says 'confidence.level set and domain_analysis populated as if the question were a valid domain specification'
Full structured JSON envelope produced with all fields (domain_analysis, business_impact, confidence) rather than a brief text deflection
Response is not concise 'a few sentences at most' — it's a complete schema output with every section filled in
